D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

Focus Stacking — Local Sharpness Selects Per Pixel

five frames at different focus distances; each output pixel takes the value from whichever frame is locally sharpest at that point

A single lens at large aperture can only focus on one depth at a time. Focus stacking takes multiple frames, each focused at a different distance, and assembles a single image with depth of field as wide as the total stack. A local-sharpness metric (high-frequency energy in a small window) decides which frame to sample at each pixel.

A scene with three subjects at three depths (foreground bottle, middle book, background lamp) is shown across five focus frames. Use the slider to pick which frame is “visible”; the right panel shows the assembled stack — every subject sharp.
